description: Following Andrew Wakefield's 1998 Lancet study linking the MMR vaccine to autism, public confidence in the UK plummeted. The crisis lasted until 2002 when the government and medical authorities launched a massive campaign to restore trust, resulting in a gradual recovery of vaccination rates.

key_events.description: Publication of Wakefield's paper in The Lancet.
National media coverage causes MMR uptake to drop below 80%.
UK government launches 'MMR: The Facts' campaign.
General Medical Council begins formal investigation into Wakefield.

calibration_notes: Exhibits a classic 'scare-driven' erosion of public trust followed by a slow, evidence-based recovery curve.
